title: Rottlera moluccana
intro: Rottlera moluccana, also known as Borneo ironwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its ability to treat fever, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic agent. The bioactive constituents of Rottlera moluccana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rottlera moluccana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Rottlera moluccana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a member of the genus Rottlera, which is a group of tropical plants. The species name Rottlera moluccana is derived from its origins in the Moluccas, an island chain in Indonesia. Rottlera moluccana is further classified as a type of evergreen shrub or small tree. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands.
uses: Rottlera moluccana has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are known to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating wounds, fever, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. Furthermore, its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand and validate its medicinal uses.

parts: Rottlera moluccana is a plant with various medicinal properties, and it is known to have several medicinal parts.
